meaning: vi.瞌睡,假寐vt.打瞌睡度过n.瞌睡
examples: Some students often doze off in class.
We couldn't help but doze off during his lecture.
I had a doze on the train.
Pretending to doze off, I let the car drift gently to the left.
The outcome of a referendum could be that "the British patient will be cured" or that it will "doze away in a state of isolation of its own choosing".
